rationale: Both outputs are concise, manuscript-ready paragraphs that preserve the fixed-budget, noisy-score, review-before-disposition structure and avoid empirical, causal, and market claims. B is more economical, but A states the supplied evidence limits precisely. B broadens the specific limitation that the model does not establish rapidly increasing harmful content into the stronger claim that it establishes no trends at all, and its reference to benign users being exposed to rejection adds an error-risk implication not specified by the model. A's allocation tradeoff remains within the fixed-capacity screening setup and therefore has better claim scope, evidence-boundary discipline, and practical usability.
